dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Your garden at a glance - {{date}}
dashboard.noBackup: No backup yet. Your data only exists in this browser.

onboarding.welcomeDesc: Your self-sufficiency garden planner. Let's set up your garden in a few quick steps.
onboarding.locationDesc: Your location helps us calculate sunlight, frost dates, and weather alerts.
onboarding.frostDesc: This date determines when to sow, transplant, and harvest. You can change it later in settings.
onboarding.gardenDesc: You can create multiple gardens later (e.g. front yard, back yard, balcony).

planner.title: Garden Planner
planner.noGarden: No garden created yet. Create one to get started!
planner.dragPlant: Drag or click to place plants
planner.confirmDeleteGarden: Delete this garden and all its beds? This cannot be undone.
planner.placeMode: Placing: {{plant}} - click on cells to place, ESC to cancel

tomato.name: Tomato
tomato.description: Warm-season crop, needs staking. Very productive in good conditions.

zucchini.name: Zucchini
zucchini.description: Prolific summer squash. One or two plants are usually enough.

carrot.name: Carrot
carrot.description: Root vegetable that prefers loose, sandy soil. Direct sow only.

lettuce.name: Lettuce
lettuce.description: Fast-growing salad green. Prefers cooler temperatures.

bean.name: Bush Bean
bean.description: Nitrogen-fixing legume. Easy to grow, productive.

pea.name: Pea
pea.description: Cool-season crop. Sow early in spring, harvest before heat.

radish.name: Radish
radish.description: Very fast-growing root vegetable. Ready in 25-30 days.

cucumber.name: Cucumber
cucumber.description: Needs warmth and consistent watering. Great for trellising.

pepper.name: Bell Pepper
pepper.description: Heat-loving plant. Start indoors early for best results.

onion.name: Onion
onion.description: Long-season crop. Start from sets or transplants for best results.

garlic.name: Garlic
garlic.description: Plant in autumn, harvest next summer. Very low maintenance.

potato.name: Potato
potato.description: Staple crop. Needs hilling as it grows. Good for beginners.

strawberry.name: Strawberry
strawberry.description: Perennial berry. Produces fruit for several years.

raspberry.name: Raspberry
raspberry.description: Perennial berry bush. Needs a trellis or support.

blueberry.name: Blueberry
blueberry.description: Needs acidic soil. Produces fruit after 2-3 years.

basil.name: Basil
basil.description: Annual herb, great companion for tomatoes. Loves warmth.

parsley.name: Parsley
parsley.description: Biennial herb. Slow to germinate but very versatile.

dill.name: Dill
dill.description: Annual herb. Attracts beneficial insects. Self-seeds readily.

chives.name: Chives
chives.description: Perennial herb with mild onion flavor. Very easy to grow.

kale.name: Kale
kale.description: Hardy leafy green. Tastes sweeter after frost.

spinach.name: Spinach
spinach.description: Cool-season green. Bolts quickly in heat.

beetroot.name: Beetroot
beetroot.description: Dual-purpose: eat both roots and leaves. Easy to grow.

leek.name: Leek
leek.description: Hardy allium. Needs blanching for white stems.

pumpkin.name: Pumpkin
pumpkin.description: Needs lots of space. Great for storage over winter.

chard.name: Swiss Chard
chard.description: Colorful, productive green. Harvest outer leaves for continuous yield.

kohlrabi.name: Kohlrabi
kohlrabi.description: Fast-growing brassica. Eat the swollen stem raw or cooked.

fennel.name: Fennel
fennel.description: Anise-flavored bulb vegetable. Does not grow well near most other plants.

corn.name: Corn
corn.description: Tall-growing cereal. Plant in blocks for good pollination.

sunflower.name: Sunflower
sunflower.description: Attracts pollinators. Edible seeds and great for companion planting.

cabbage.name: Cabbage
cabbage.description: Cool-season brassica. Good for sauerkraut and storage.

broccoli.name: Broccoli
broccoli.description: Harvest the main head, then side shoots keep producing.

cauliflower.name: Cauliflower
cauliflower.description: Needs consistent conditions. Blanch heads by tying leaves.

celery.name: Celery
celery.description: Long-season crop that needs rich soil and consistent moisture.

turnip.name: Turnip
turnip.description: Quick-growing root vegetable. Both roots and greens are edible.

mint.name: Mint
mint.description: Vigorous perennial herb. Best grown in containers to prevent spreading.

rosemary.name: Rosemary
rosemary.description: Woody perennial herb. Drought-tolerant once established.

thyme.name: Thyme
thyme.description: Low-growing perennial herb. Excellent ground cover.

currant.name: Currant
currant.description: Hardy berry bush. Red and black varieties available.

gooseberry.name: Gooseberry
gooseberry.description: Thorny berry bush. Excellent for jams and desserts.

eggplant.name: Eggplant
eggplant.description: Heat-loving solanaceous fruit. Needs warm conditions and rich soil.

arugula.name: Arugula
arugula.description: Peppery salad green. Fast-growing cool-season crop, ready in weeks.

squash.name: Butternut Squash
squash.description: Winter squash with sweet, nutty flesh. Stores well for months.

asparagus.name: Asparagus
asparagus.description: Perennial vegetable. Takes 2-3 years to establish but produces for 15+ years.

pak_choi.name: Pak Choi
pak_choi.description: Fast-growing Asian green with crisp white stems and tender leaves.

endive.name: Endive
endive.description: Slightly bitter salad green. Blanch hearts for milder flavor.

weather.title: Weather
weather.noApiKey: Please add your OpenWeatherMap API key in Settings.
weather.noLocation: Please set your location in Settings.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.noTasks: No tasks yet. Plan your garden to generate tasks automatically.

settings.title: Settings
settings.backendHint: Leave empty for local-only mode. Set when running in Docker.

dataManagement.title: Data Management
dataManagement.clearConfirm: Delete ALL data? This cannot be undone. Make a backup first!
dataManagement.noBackup: No backup created yet. Your data only exists in this browser!
dataManagement.invalidFile: Invalid backup file. Must be a Gardener export.
dataManagement.importModeDesc: Merge adds new data without deleting existing. Overwrite replaces everything.

alerts.frostDesc: Temperatures dropping to {{temp}}°C on {{date}}. Protect sensitive plants!
alerts.greenhouseColdDesc: {{name}} may drop to {{temp}}°C on {{date}} (min: {{min}}°C). Consider heating.
alerts.wateringHotDry: Hot and dry days ahead ({{temp}}°C avg). Water deeply in the morning.
alerts.wateringRainExpected: Rain expected in the next days. Hold off on watering.
alerts.wateringHighNeedPlants: {{count}} high-water plants need attention. No rain in forecast.
alerts.weeklySummaryNeutral: Moderate conditions ahead. Temps {{minTemp}}–{{maxTemp}}°C, {{rainyDays}} rainy days.
alerts.weeklySummaryFrost: Frost risk this week! Min {{minTemp}}°C. Protect tender plants and delay transplanting.
alerts.weeklySummaryHotDry: Hot and dry week ahead (up to {{maxTemp}}°C). Prioritize watering and mulching.
alerts.weeklySummaryWet: Wet week expected ({{rainyDays}} rainy days). Good for new plantings, watch for slugs.
alerts.weeklySummaryWarm: Warm week ahead ({{maxTemp}}°C). Great growing conditions!

harvest.title: Harvest Log
harvest.noEntries: No harvests logged yet. Start tracking your yields!

journal.title: Garden Journal
journal.text: What happened in the garden today?
journal.noEntries: Your garden journal is empty. Start documenting your garden journey!

season.archiveConfirm: Archive the current season and start fresh? All beds will be cleared but your harvest data and journal remain.

sufficiency.title: Self-Sufficiency Calculator
sufficiency.noPlantings: Plan your garden first to see self-sufficiency calculations.
sufficiency.coverageDesc: Estimated coverage of nutritional needs for {{size}} people during the growing season (180 days).

preservation.title: Preservation Guide
preservation.desc: Recommended preservation methods for your planted crops.

strategy.balancedDesc: Diverse selection of vegetables, herbs, and more
strategy.caloriesDesc: Potato, corn, beans - highest calorie output per m²
strategy.selfsufficientDesc: Cover all nutrients: calories, protein, vitamins, fiber + preservable
strategy.yieldDesc: Tomato, zucchini, cucumber - most kg per m²
strategy.beginnerDesc: Easy to grow, low maintenance, direct sow, fast results
strategy.quickharvestDesc: Radish, lettuce, spinach - harvest in weeks, not months

validation.antagonistDirect: {{neighbor}} should not be directly next to {{plant}}!
validation.antagonistNear: {{neighbor}} is close to {{plant}} - not ideal neighbors
validation.tooClose: Too close to same plant (need {{spacing}}cm, only {{actual}}cm)
validation.tooMuchSun: This plant prefers shade - may get too much sun here
validation.containerTooSmall: This container might be too small for this plant

expenses.title: Costs & ROI
expenses.noEntries: No expenses tracked yet. Start logging your garden costs!

succession.title: Succession Planting
succession.desc: Stagger your sowings for continuous harvest throughout the season.

threeSisters.name: Three Sisters
threeSisters.desc: Traditional: Corn + Beans + Squash support each other.

tomatoBasil.name: Tomato & Basil
tomatoBasil.desc: Classic Mediterranean pairing with carrots and parsley.

saladBed.name: Salad Bed
saladBed.desc: Quick-growing salad mix with radish, spinach and herbs.

mediterranean.name: Mediterranean
mediterranean.desc: Sun-loving mix: tomato, pepper, zucchini with herbs.

rootVegetables.name: Root Vegetables
rootVegetables.desc: Underground harvest: carrots, beets, turnips with alliums.

brassica.name: Brassica Guild
brassica.desc: Cabbage family with protective herbs and root vegetables.

common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this?
